label: Events Feed
description: Streaming feed of platform and market events: order lifecycle, risk alerts, and system notifications.

0.5.note: No import from lib/mocks/ — widget consumes events from useTerminalData(); source is MOCK_TERMINAL_EVENTS in lib/config/services/terminal-events.config.ts.

0.6.note: isLoading guard renders 'Loading events…' with animate-pulse.

0.7.note: events.length===0 guard renders 'No events to display'.

0.8.note: error guard renders 'Failed to load events: {error}'.

0.9.note: Inline const retired; events now sourced from MOCK_TERMINAL_EVENTS in lib/config/services/terminal-events.config.ts.

l0.notes: All L0 checks pass. Fixes applied in previous session.

1.1.note: terminal-data-context.tsx now exposes events/isLoadingEvents/errorEvents (TerminalEvent[] + TerminalEventDomain/Severity types). use-terminal-page-data.ts serves MOCK_TERMINAL_EVENTS from lib/config/services/terminal-events.config.ts with isLoadingEvents=false/errorEvents=null until event bus API is wired. Widget reads these via useTerminalData().

1.2.note: Events source is context-only; no direct mock imports in widget file.

1.3.note: Cross-tab (terminal/overview/strategies) reads same context; placeholder source is scope-agnostic. Scope filtering deferred to real event bus wiring.

1.4.note: Placeholder source is not client-scoped; real event bus will honor scope client filters when wired.

1.5.note: Placeholder source is not strategy-scoped; real event bus will honor scope strategy filters when wired.

1.6.note: Empty scope falls back to the full placeholder array — behavior matches other terminal widgets.

l1.notes: L1 passes after context wiring. Events/isLoadingEvents/errorEvents plumbed through useTerminalData(); widget no longer defines its own placeholder array. Real event-bus subscription is the next wiring step but is out of scope for L1 context compliance.

l2.notes: Covered globally by commits 32ba8ab and f9f29ea. Re-audit only if wiring test fails.

3.1.note: Modes: live ✓ (events should stream in real-time), batch — expected behavior: show historical events up to asOf datetime. Currently using placeholder data so no mode distinction implemented.

3.2.note: Widget now reads events/isLoadingEvents/errorEvents from useTerminalData(). Context-side mode switching will propagate once the events source is wired to a real bus; widget itself no longer hardcodes data.

3.5.note: Paper mode expected: same as live (read-only event stream). No behavioral change needed.

3.6.note: No WebSocket or timed interval in widget file. No real-time connection to disable — placeholder events are static.

l3.notes: Agent checks pass after L1 context wiring. Real mode-aware event source (WS/SSE vs batch snapshot) still depends on backend owner resolution.

4.1.note: No buttons in this widget — read-only event list.

4.6.note: No entitlement checks in render path.

l4.notes: All agent checks pass (read-only display widget). Human checks 4.8(scroll within flex overflow-auto) required.

5.1.note: Target strategies: all — events feed shows system-wide events across EXECUTION, RISK, DATA, SYSTEM, STRATEGY domains. Relevant to all strategy types.

l5.notes: 5.1 complete. Human checks 5.2–5.6 required. Context wiring landed; real event-source readiness still depends on backend owner.

l6.notes: L1.5 harness spec: 11 tests covering render (titles, timestamps, domain labels, severity badges, count badge), loading/error/empty states, multi-event WARNING+CRITICAL severity. See tests/widgets/terminal/events-feed.test.tsx

7.1.note: No hardcoded hex/rgb/hsl in widget file. CLEAN.

7.4.note: EventsFeedWidget PascalCase; severityBadgeVariant and domainColor are private helper functions (lowercase first letter is acceptable for non-exported helpers). File kebab-case. CLEAN.

7.5.note: Out of scope — English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21; 7.5 reassigned to UTC datetime rendering

7.6.note: No dangerouslySetInnerHTML, eval, new Function. Template literals safe (static domain color names, not user input). CLEAN.

7.7.note: No useEffect/useMemo/useCallback in widget file. CLEAN.

l7.notes: L7 pass. All mechanical checks clean; L1 context wiring now complete.

8.1.note: text-[10px] → text-micro at lines 155, 165, 168, 174; text-[9px] → text-nano at line 169. Tokens now available in globals.css @theme.

8.3.note: Uses Badge from components/ui/badge. Event rows use div layout (appropriate — no Button/Card semantics needed for list rows). CLEAN.

8.4.note: Agent: no img, no input, no interactive div (event rows are display-only divs, not clickable). Human: keyboard + screen-reader pass required.

8.5.note: Human check required — responsive at minW=4, minH=3. Available on terminal, overview, strategies tabs.

keepSeparate.rationale: Events feed is a cross-domain system event stream — not driven by instrument or strategy selection. It spans 3 tabs (terminal, overview, strategies) as a standalone monitoring panel.

relationships.notes: Standalone widget. Available on terminal, overview, and strategies tabs. Events are now consumed from useTerminalData(); domain-level filtering by active scope is a follow-up once the real event bus is wired.

dataContract.mockSource: lib/config/services/terminal-events.config.ts MOCK_TERMINAL_EVENTS (10 static entries — served through TerminalData.events until real event bus is wired)
dataContract.backendOwner: unknown — likely unified-events-interface or a dedicated event-bus service
dataContract.notes: Context-wired: widget consumes events/isLoadingEvents/errorEvents from useTerminalData(). Source is still placeholder; production path: WS or SSE from platform event bus. Events span EXECUTION, RISK, DATA, SYSTEM, STRATEGY domains. Backend owner TBD.

gaps: Source is still MOCK_TERMINAL_EVENTS in lib/config/services/terminal-events.config.ts — real WS/SSE event bus not wired
No pagination or virtual scrolling for high-volume event streams
coverage.notes: Context wiring complete; real event bus still pending. Critical gap for monitoring readiness remains until backend owner resolves.

knownIssues: Events source is still MOCK_TERMINAL_EVENTS in lib/config/services/terminal-events.config.ts — replace with real WS/SSE subscription once event bus owner is resolved
No virtual scrolling — will performance-degrade with high-volume event streams

findings.description: events-feed-widget.tsx — events sourced from inline const; terminal-data-context.tsx missing events/isLoadingEvents/errorEvents fields
findings.fix: Extended terminal-data-context.tsx with TerminalEvent[]/isLoadingEvents/errorEvents (plus TerminalEventDomain/TerminalEventSeverity types). Promoted source to lib/config/services/terminal-events.config.ts as MOCK_TERMINAL_EVENTS and exposed it through TerminalData via use-terminal-page-data.ts. Widget now calls useTerminalData() and renders spinner/error states from errorEvents/isLoadingEvents.

findings.description: events-feed-widget.tsx — ~13 user-visible strings not i18n wrapped
findings.fix: Retired 7.5 i18n check: English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21

findings.description: events-feed-widget.tsx:155,165,168,169,174 — text-[10px]/text-[9px] arbitrary typography with no matching globals.css token
findings.fix: Applied text-micro (10px), text-nano (9px), text-caption (11px) tokens per consolidated globals.css; arbitrary text-[Npx] classes removed
